title: pillowy Brazilian renkon-lotus-root Confit
description: a resinous, pillowy sick-day dish featuring renkon-lotus-root, confit-tomato, and ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients.

ingredients.name: renkon-lotus-root
ingredients.notes: any firm, fibrous root will do

ingredients.name: confit-tomato
ingredients.notes: use a flavorful, acidic tomato confit

instructions: preheat the oven to 3006 fahrenheit, or 1506 celsius.
slice the renkon-lotus-root into thin, uniform pieces.
mix the confit-tomato, garlic, ginger, lemon juice, cumin powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
add the renkon-lotus-root slices to the bowl, and toss to coat with the confit-tomato mixture.
heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add the renkon-lotus-root slices to the skillet, and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until tender and lightly browned.
transfer the skillet to the preheated oven, and bake for 20-25 minutes, until the renkon-lotus-root is tender and the liquid has reduced.
remove the skillet from the oven, and let cool slightly.
serve the renkon-lotus-root confit warm, garnished with fresh herbs or edible flowers, if desired.
